title: Shell-and-Tube Heat Exchanger NTU Effectiveness Logger
description: Continuously measures and logs the thermal effectiveness and NTU of a small shell-and-tube heat exchanger using four DS18B20 probes (hot inlet/outlet, cold inlet/outlet) and a Hall-effect flow sensor. Arduino computes ε and NTU every 5 s and logs to SD card. A relay-switched pump maintains circulation. Fault triggers if hot-side outlet exceeds cold-side inlet (cross-over). Enables real-time fouling detection over time.
objective: Log heat exchanger effectiveness ε and NTU at 5 s intervals, detect fouling-induced ε degradation over a 2-hour run, and flag flow faults.
constraints: Arduino Uno, four DS18B20 probes, YF-S201 flow sensor, relay pump control, SD logger; water-only fluid; max temperature 80 °C; max flow 5 L/min.

element_usage_notes.DS18B20 1-Wire Temperature Probe Array: Four probes on 1-Wire bus with individual ROM addresses measuring hot/cold inlet and outlet temperatures.
element_usage_notes.Hall-Effect Flow Rate Sensor: Mounted on hot-side supply line; pulse count per second converted to L/min for heat duty calculation.
element_usage_notes.Relay-Switched Magnetic Stirrer: Relay repurposed to switch circulation pump ON/OFF; pump keeps both fluid loops moving.
element_usage_notes.SD Card Data Logger Module: Logs t, Th_in, Th_out, Tc_in, Tc_out, flow_L_min, effectiveness, NTU every 5 s.

element_interactions.description: Hot/cold inlet/outlet temperatures logged every measurement cycle
Flow rate logged alongside temperatures for heat duty calculation
Temperature cross-over fault triggers pump shutdown via relay
Flow confirmation required before temperature readings are trusted for NTU calc
